data.title: Natural Gas Drillers Aim to Eliminate Middlemen for Increased Profits
data.description: Expand Energy Corp. and EQT Corp. are seeking to bypass traders who act as intermediaries in the natural gas market. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the efforts of these major U.S. natural gas drillers to enhance their profitability by directly managing their output. This strategic move aims to streamline operations and maximize revenue by reducing reliance on third-party traders.

data.title: Rising MBA Tuition Amid Increasing Costs for Business Schools
data.description: MBA tuition fees are on the rise as business schools grapple with escalating salaries, staffing expenses, and competitive pressures. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the financial challenges faced by these institutions. The increase in tuition is attributed to the need to attract and retain top faculty, as well as to enhance facilities and programs to remain competitive in the global education market. As schools strive to offer cutting-edge curricula and maintain their reputations, students are bearing the brunt of these financial demands through higher tuition costs. This trend reflects broader economic pressures impacting higher education institutions worldwide.

data.title: NASA Plans Mars Mission Launch by End of 2028
data.description: NASA has announced its intention to launch a mission to Mars by the end of 2028. According to Jin10, this ambitious plan aims to further explore the Red Planet and gather valuable scientific data. The mission is part of NASA's ongoing efforts to expand human understanding of Mars and its potential for future exploration. Details about the specific objectives and technologies involved in the mission are yet to be disclosed, but the announcement marks a significant step in space exploration.

data.title: UK and Switzerland Progress in Free-Trade Agreement Talks
data.description: The United Kingdom and Switzerland are making significant progress in their negotiations for a new free-trade agreement. Bloomberg posted on X that the Swiss government highlighted the need to address key issues such as data regulation, intellectual property, and access to service-sector companies. Despite these challenges, the discussions are described as 'well advanced,' indicating a positive trajectory towards finalizing the agreement.

data.title: Solana Foundation launches SDP, a developer platform supporting AI.
data.description: The Solana Foundation announced the launch of the Solana Developer Platform (SDP), a platform for enterprises and financial institutions that supports the building and deployment of Solana-based financial products via APIs. SDP integrates node infrastructure, wallets, compliance, and deposit/withdrawal services, providing modules for token issuance, payments, and trading. The issuance and payment modules are already online, while the trading module is expected to launch in 2026. Mastercard, Worldpay, and Western Union are among the first users, exploring application scenarios such as stablecoin settlement, merchant payments, and cross-border payments.

data.title: Tether partners with one of the Big Four accounting firms to conduct its first full audit.
data.description: Tether has announced a formal partnership with one of the Big Four accounting firms to conduct its first independent and comprehensive financial audit. This audit will cover its digital asset reserves, traditional assets, and tokenized liabilities, aiming to improve transparency and verify the liquidity and risk management of the USDT reserves. Tether stated that this move marks a step towards a full audit rather than just periodic reports.

data.title: NASA Cancels Lunar Gateway Project to Focus on Moon Base Construction
data.description: NASA Administrator has announced the cancellation of the planned Lunar Gateway space station project, shifting focus towards establishing a base on the lunar surface. According to Jin10, this strategic change aims to enhance lunar exploration capabilities and support future missions. The decision reflects NASA's commitment to advancing human presence on the Moon, prioritizing direct surface operations over orbital infrastructure. This move is expected to facilitate more extensive scientific research and exploration activities on the Moon, aligning with NASA's long-term goals for lunar development.

data.title: Saudi Prince Communicates Message to U.S. President Trump, New York Times Reports
data.description: Saudi Prince recently conveyed a message to U.S. President Donald Trump during discussions, according to Jin10. The New York Times reported that the communication took place amid ongoing diplomatic engagements between the two nations. The specifics of the message remain undisclosed, but it underscores the continued dialogue between Saudi Arabia and the United States. This interaction highlights the strategic importance of bilateral relations in addressing regional and global issues. Further details on the content of the message or the outcomes of the meeting have not been provided.

data.title: Hyperliquid's Revenue and Buyback Activity Update
data.description: Hyperliquid reported generating $2.32 million in revenue yesterday. According to NS3.AI, the Hyperliquid Foundation has actively engaged in buyback activities over the past week, purchasing 237,460 HYPE tokens valued at $11.08 million through its Assistance Fund. This fund now holds 14.22% of HYPE's circulating supply.

data.title: India Extends Gold Import Duty Quota Under UAE Agreement Until June 30
data.description: India has announced an extension of the validity period for the gold import duty quota under the India-UAE Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement framework. According to Jin10, the authorization for this quota will now remain effective until June 30. This move is part of ongoing economic collaboration between India and the United Arab Emirates, aimed at facilitating trade and investment between the two nations.

data.title: Crispin Odey Reflects on Perceptions of His Behavior in Court Testimony
data.description: Crispin Odey, a prominent figure in the financial sector, addressed a London court regarding his past behavior. Bloomberg posted on X that Odey acknowledged that while his actions might have been deemed acceptable in his youth, he recognized that by the time he reached his 60s, women might perceive him as a 'creepy old man.' Odey's reflections come amid ongoing legal proceedings, highlighting the evolving perceptions of personal conduct over time.

data.title: Tether Engages Major Audit Firm for Comprehensive Audit
data.description: Tether has announced the engagement of a major audit firm, known as one of the 'Big Four', to conduct its first full audit. According to BWEnews, this move marks a significant step for Tether in enhancing transparency and trust within the cryptocurrency industry. The audit is expected to provide a comprehensive review of Tether's financials, addressing long-standing questions about its reserves and financial practices. This development is seen as a positive move towards greater accountability and could influence other stablecoin issuers to follow suit.

data.title: Market news: Iran is charging security fees to some ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: Market sources indicate that Iran is charging "security passage fees" to some ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. President Trump Discusses Middle East Situation with India's Prime Minister Modi
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump recently held a phone conversation with India's Prime Minister Narendra Modi to discuss the ongoing situation in the Middle East. According to Jin10, the discussion emphasized the importance of keeping the Strait of Hormuz open. This strategic waterway is crucial for global oil transportation, and its accessibility remains a significant concern amid regional tensions.

data.title: UK Chancellor Reeves: Contingency Plans Underway for Various Scenarios
data.description: UK Chancellor Rachel Reeves has announced that the government is actively preparing contingency plans to address a range of potential situations. According to Jin10, Reeves emphasized the importance of being ready for unforeseen events that could impact the country's economic stability. The Chancellor's remarks come amid ongoing discussions about the UK's financial strategies and the need for robust measures to safeguard against economic uncertainties. Reeves highlighted the government's commitment to ensuring that the UK remains resilient in the face of global challenges, stressing the significance of proactive planning in maintaining economic security. The announcement reflects the government's focus on adaptability and preparedness in navigating complex economic landscapes.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Robert Kiyosaki Advocates Silver Investment Amid Price Decline
data.description: Robert Kiyosaki, a prominent financial educator, has encouraged investors to consider purchasing silver following a significant drop in its price. According to NS3.AI, the XAG/USD rate has decreased from $122 in January to approximately $67 currently. Kiyosaki believes that silver is nearing its bottom and has the potential to eventually reach $200. He also suggested that individuals with limited financial resources could skip a meal and use the saved $10 to invest in junk silver coins.

data.title: Mexico's Inflation Surge Heightens Pressure on Central Bank's Rate Decision
data.description: Mexico's annual inflation rate increased significantly in early March, surpassing expectations and intensifying the focus on the central bank's upcoming interest rate meeting. Bloomberg posted on X that the unexpected rise in inflation could influence the central bank's decision-making process as it seeks to balance economic growth with price stability. The central bank's meeting on Thursday will be closely watched by analysts and investors, as they anticipate potential adjustments to interest rates in response to the inflationary pressures. The acceleration in inflation underscores the challenges faced by policymakers in managing the country's economic outlook amid fluctuating global conditions.

data.title: UK Chancellor Reeves: Fiscal Rules Are 'Iron Laws'
data.description: UK Chancellor Rachel Reeves has emphasized the importance of fiscal rules, describing them as 'iron laws' that guide the country's economic policies. According to Jin10, Reeves highlighted the necessity of adhering to these rules to ensure economic stability and growth. Her statement underscores the government's commitment to maintaining fiscal discipline amid ongoing economic challenges. Reeves' remarks come at a time when the UK is navigating complex financial landscapes, and her emphasis on fiscal rules reflects a strategic approach to managing the nation's economy.

data.title: Nvidia in Talks to Invest in AI Startup Sarvam
data.description: Nvidia is reportedly in discussions to invest in the artificial intelligence startup Sarvam. According to PANews, the negotiations are ongoing as Nvidia seeks to expand its presence in the AI sector. The potential investment highlights Nvidia's continued interest in fostering innovation and growth within the AI industry.

data.title: Eversendai's Contract for Saudi Arabia's Neom Project Terminated
data.description: Malaysia’s Eversendai Corporation Bhd. announced the termination of its structural steel contract for Trojena Ski Village, a segment of Saudi Arabia’s ambitious Neom megacity project. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the decision amid rising tensions in the region. The termination marks a significant development in the ongoing challenges faced by the Neom project, which aims to transform the area into a futuristic urban hub. Eversendai's involvement in the project was part of a broader effort to contribute to the construction of the innovative ski village, envisioned as a key attraction within Neom. The reasons behind the contract termination have not been detailed, but the geopolitical climate in the region is believed to have influenced the decision. Eversendai's exit from the project underscores the complexities and uncertainties associated with large-scale developments in volatile areas.
